```{r}
  test_1$type <- factor(
    test_1$type,
    levels = levels(train_1$type)
  )

  test_1$ white <- factor(
    test_1$ white,
    levels = levels(train_1$ white)
  )

  #====================================
  # MODEL MATRICES
  #====================================

  X_train1 <- model.matrix(

    los ~ hmo + died + type +  white,

    data = train_1
  )[,-1]

  X_test1 <- model.matrix(

    los ~ hmo + died + type +  white,

    data = test_1
  )[,-1]

  y_train1 <- train_1$los

  y_test1 <- test_1$los

  #====================================
  # FIT GLMNET MODEL
  #====================================

  model_glmnet1 <- cv.glmnet(

    x = X_train1,

    y = y_train1,

    family = "poisson",

    alpha = 1
  )

  #====================================
  # POINT PREDICTION
  #====================================

  pred_glmnet1 <- as.numeric(

    predict(

      model_glmnet1,

      newx = X_test1,

      s = "lambda.min",

      type = "response"
    )
  )

  pred_glmnet1_round <- round(
    pred_glmnet1
  )

  actual1 <- y_test1

  #====================================
  # REMOVE INVALID VALUES
  #====================================

  keep <- complete.cases(
    actual1,
    pred_glmnet1
  )

  actual1 <- actual1[keep]

  pred_glmnet1 <- pred_glmnet1[keep]

  pred_glmnet1_round <- pred_glmnet1_round[keep]

  if(length(actual1) < 2) next

  #====================================
  # FORECAST ACCURACY
  #====================================

  acc_glmnet1 <- forecast::accuracy(

    pred_glmnet1,

    actual1
  )

  #===========================================
  # TRAIN PREDICTIONS
  #===========================================

  train_pred_glmnet1 <- as.numeric(

    predict(

      model_glmnet1,

      newx = X_train1,

      s = "lambda.min",

      type = "response"
    )
  )

  #===========================================
  # RESIDUALS
  #===========================================

  resid_glmnet1 <- (

    y_train1 -

      train_pred_glmnet1
  )

  #===========================================
  # BOOTSTRAP
  #===========================================

  B <- 1000

  pred_matrix_glmnet1 <- matrix(

    NA,

    nrow = length(pred_glmnet1),

    ncol = B
  )

  #===========================================
  # RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP
  #===========================================

  for(b in 1:B){

    sampled_resid1 <- sample(

      resid_glmnet1,

      size = length(pred_glmnet1),

      replace = TRUE
    )

    pred_matrix_glmnet1[, b] <- (

      pred_glmnet1 +

        sampled_resid1
    )
  }

  #===========================================
  # PI
  #===========================================

  lower_glmnet1 <- apply(

    pred_matrix_glmnet1,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.025
  )

  upper_glmnet1 <- apply(

    pred_matrix_glmnet1,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.975
  )

  #===========================================
  # NONNEGATIVE COUNTS
  #===========================================

  lower_glmnet1 <- pmax(

    0,

    round(lower_glmnet1)
  )

  upper_glmnet1 <- round(

    upper_glmnet1
  )

  #===========================================
  # COVERAGE
  #===========================================

  coverage_glmnet1 <- mean(

    actual1 >= lower_glmnet1 &

    actual1 <= upper_glmnet1
  )

  #===========================================
  # WIDTH
  #===========================================

  width_glmnet1 <- mean(

    upper_glmnet1 -

      lower_glmnet1
  )

  #====================================
  # STORE EACH SPLIT
  #====================================

  split_glmnet1 <- data.frame(

    Actual = actual1,

    Predicted = pred_glmnet1,

    Lower_PI = lower_glmnet1,

    Upper_PI = upper_glmnet1
  )

  assign(

    paste0(
      "GLMNET1_SPLIT_",
      i
    ),

    split_glmnet1
  )

  #====================================
  # STORE RESULTS
  #====================================

  results_glmnet1 <- rbind(

    results_glmnet1,

    data.frame(

      Split = i,

      ME = acc_glmnet1[1,"ME"],

      RMSE = acc_glmnet1[1,"RMSE"],

      MAE = acc_glmnet1[1,"MAE"],

      MPE = acc_glmnet1[1,"MPE"],

      MAPE = acc_glmnet1[1,"MAPE"],

      Coverage = coverage_glmnet1,

      Width = width_glmnet1
    )
  )

  #====================================
  # DISPLAY RESULTS
  #====================================

  cat("\n")

  cat(
    "Coverage :",
    coverage_glmnet1,
    "\n"
  )

  cat(
    "Width :",
    width_glmnet1,
    "\n"
  )
}
```

```{r}
#====================================
# SUMMARY TABLE
#====================================

summary_glmnet1 <- data.frame(

  Model = "GLMNET1",

  Split = "70%",

  Mean_ME = mean(
    results_glmnet1$ME,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_RMSE = mean(
    results_glmnet1$RMSE,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_MAE = mean(
    results_glmnet1$MAE,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_MPE = mean(
    results_glmnet1$MPE,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_MAPE = mean(
    results_glmnet1$MAPE,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_Coverage = mean(
    results_glmnet1$Coverage,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_Width = mean(
    results_glmnet1$Width,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
)

summary_glmnet1
```

## 80 split
```{r}
#========================================
# GLMNET2 : 80% SPLITS
# ELASTIC NET / GLMNET
# RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP PI
#========================================

library(glmnet)
library(forecast)

results_glmnet2 <- data.frame()

#========================================
# LOOP
#========================================

for(i in 1:100){

  cat("\n====================================\n")
  cat("GLMNET2 : SPLIT", i, "\n")
  cat("====================================\n")

  #====================================
  # TRAIN AND TEST
  #====================================

  train_2 <- split_80[[i]]$train

  test_2 <- split_80[[i]]$test

  #====================================
  # FACTOR VARIABLES
  #====================================

  train_2$hmo <- factor(train_2$hmo)

  train_2$died <- factor(train_2$died)

  train_2$type <- factor(train_2$type)

  train_2$ white <- factor(train_2$ white)

  test_2$hmo <- factor(
    test_2$hmo,
    levels = levels(train_2$hmo)
  )

  test_2$died <- factor(
    test_2$died,
    levels = levels(train_2$died)
  )

  test_2$type <- factor(
    test_2$type,
    levels = levels(train_2$type)
  )

  test_2$ white <- factor(
    test_2$ white,
    levels = levels(train_2$ white)
  )

  #====================================
  # MODEL MATRICES
  #====================================

  X_train2 <- model.matrix(

    los ~ hmo + died + type + white,

    data = train_2
  )[,-1]

  X_test2 <- model.matrix(

    los ~ hmo + died + type + white,

    data = test_2
  )[,-1]

  y_train2 <- train_2$los

  y_test2 <- test_2$los

  #====================================
  # FIT GLMNET MODEL
  #====================================

  model_glmnet2 <- cv.glmnet(

    x = X_train2,

    y = y_train2,

    family = "poisson",

    alpha = 1
  )

  #====================================
  # POINT PREDICTION
  #====================================

  pred_glmnet2 <- as.numeric(

    predict(

      model_glmnet2,

      newx = X_test2,

      s = "lambda.min",

      type = "response"
    )
  )

  pred_glmnet2_round <- round(
    pred_glmnet2
  )

  actual2 <- y_test2

  #====================================
  # REMOVE INVALID VALUES
  #====================================

  keep <- complete.cases(
    actual2,
    pred_glmnet2
  )

  actual2 <- actual2[keep]

  pred_glmnet2 <- pred_glmnet2[keep]

  pred_glmnet2_round <- pred_glmnet2_round[keep]

  if(length(actual2) < 2) next

  #====================================
  # FORECAST ACCURACY
  #====================================

  acc_glmnet2 <- forecast::accuracy(

    pred_glmnet2,

    actual2
  )

  #===========================================
  # TRAIN PREDICTIONS
  #===========================================

  train_pred_glmnet2 <- as.numeric(

    predict(

      model_glmnet2,

      newx = X_train2,

      s = "lambda.min",

      type = "response"
    )
  )

  #===========================================
  # RESIDUALS
  #===========================================

  resid_glmnet2 <- (

    y_train2 -

      train_pred_glmnet2
  )

  #===========================================
  # BOOTSTRAP
  #===========================================

  B <- 1000

  pred_matrix_glmnet2 <- matrix(

    NA,

    nrow = length(pred_glmnet2),

    ncol = B
  )

  #===========================================
  # RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP
  #===========================================

  for(b in 1:B){

    sampled_resid2 <- sample(

      resid_glmnet2,

      size = length(pred_glmnet2),

      replace = TRUE
    )

    pred_matrix_glmnet2[, b] <- (

      pred_glmnet2 +

        sampled_resid2
    )
  }

  #===========================================
  # PI
  #===========================================

  lower_glmnet2 <- apply(

    pred_matrix_glmnet2,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.025
  )

  upper_glmnet2 <- apply(

    pred_matrix_glmnet2,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.975
  )

  #===========================================
  # NONNEGATIVE COUNTS
  #===========================================

  lower_glmnet2 <- pmax(

    0,

    round(lower_glmnet2)
  )

  upper_glmnet2 <- round(

    upper_glmnet2
  )

  #===========================================
  # COVERAGE
  #===========================================

  coverage_glmnet2 <- mean(

    actual2 >= lower_glmnet2 &

    actual2 <= upper_glmnet2
  )

  #===========================================
  # WIDTH
  #===========================================

  width_glmnet2 <- mean(

    upper_glmnet2 -

      lower_glmnet2
  )

  #====================================
  # STORE RESULTS
  #====================================

  results_glmnet2 <- rbind(

    results_glmnet2,

    data.frame(

      Split = i,

      ME = acc_glmnet2[1,"ME"],

      RMSE = acc_glmnet2[1,"RMSE"],

      MAE = acc_glmnet2[1,"MAE"],

      MPE = acc_glmnet2[1,"MPE"],

      MAPE = acc_glmnet2[1,"MAPE"],

      Coverage = coverage_glmnet2,

      Width = width_glmnet2
    )
  )
}
```


```{r}
#====================================
# SUMMARY TABLE
#====================================

summary_glmnet2 <- data.frame(

  Model = "GLMNET2",

  Split = "80%",

  Mean_ME = mean(results_glmnet2$ME,na.rm = TRUE),

  Mean_RMSE = mean(results_glmnet2$RMSE,na.rm = TRUE),

  Mean_MAE = mean(results_glmnet2$MAE,na.rm = TRUE),

  Mean_MPE = mean(results_glmnet2$MPE,na.rm = TRUE),

  Mean_MAPE = mean(results_glmnet2$MAPE,na.rm = TRUE),

  Mean_Coverage = mean(results_glmnet2$Coverage,na.rm = TRUE),

  Mean_Width = mean(results_glmnet2$Width,na.rm = TRUE)
)

summary_glmnet2
```

## 90 split
```{r}
#========================================
# GLMNET3 : 90% SPLITS
# ELASTIC NET / GLMNET
# RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP PI
#========================================

library(glmnet)
library(forecast)

results_glmnet3 <- data.frame()

#========================================
# LOOP
#========================================

for(i in 1:100){

  cat("\n====================================\n")
  cat("GLMNET3 : SPLIT", i, "\n")
  cat("====================================\n")

  #====================================
  # TRAIN AND TEST
  #====================================

  train_3 <- split_90[[i]]$train

  test_3 <- split_90[[i]]$test

  #====================================
  # FACTOR VARIABLES
  #====================================

  train_3$hmo <- factor(train_3$hmo)

  train_3$died <- factor(train_3$died)

  train_3$type <- factor(train_3$type)

  train_3$ white<- factor(train_3$ white)

  test_3$hmo <- factor(
    test_3$hmo,
    levels = levels(train_3$hmo)
  )

  test_3$died <- factor(
    test_3$died,
    levels = levels(train_3$died)
  )

  test_3$type <- factor(
    test_3$type,
    levels = levels(train_3$type)
  )

  test_3$ white <- factor(
    test_3$ white,
    levels = levels(train_3$ white)
  )

  #====================================
  # MODEL MATRICES
  #====================================

  X_train3 <- model.matrix(

    los ~ hmo + died + type + white,

    data = train_3
  )[,-1]

  X_test3 <- model.matrix(

    los ~ hmo + died + type +  white,

    data = test_3
  )[,-1]

  y_train3 <- train_3$los

  y_test3 <- test_3$los

  #====================================
  # FIT GLMNET MODEL
  #====================================

  model_glmnet3 <- cv.glmnet(

    x = X_train3,

    y = y_train3,

    family = "poisson",

    alpha = 1
  )

  #====================================
  # POINT PREDICTION
  #====================================

  pred_glmnet3 <- as.numeric(

    predict(

      model_glmnet3,

      newx = X_test3,

      s = "lambda.min",

      type = "response"
    )
  )

  pred_glmnet3_round <- round(
    pred_glmnet3
  )

  actual3 <- y_test3

  #====================================
  # REMOVE INVALID VALUES
  #====================================

  keep <- complete.cases(
    actual3,
    pred_glmnet3
  )

  actual3 <- actual3[keep]

  pred_glmnet3 <- pred_glmnet3[keep]

  pred_glmnet3_round <- pred_glmnet3_round[keep]

  if(length(actual3) < 2) next

  #====================================
  # FORECAST ACCURACY
  #====================================

  acc_glmnet3 <- forecast::accuracy(

    pred_glmnet3,

    actual3
  )

  #===========================================
  # TRAIN PREDICTIONS
  #===========================================

  train_pred_glmnet3 <- as.numeric(

    predict(

      model_glmnet3,

      newx = X_train3,

      s = "lambda.min",

      type = "response"
    )
  )

  #===========================================
  # RESIDUALS
  #===========================================

  resid_glmnet3 <- (

    y_train3 -

      train_pred_glmnet3
  )

  #===========================================
  # BOOTSTRAP
  #===========================================

  B <- 1000

  pred_matrix_glmnet3 <- matrix(

    NA,

    nrow = length(pred_glmnet3),

    ncol = B
  )

  #===========================================
  # RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP
  #===========================================

  for(b in 1:B){

    sampled_resid3 <- sample(

      resid_glmnet3,

      size = length(pred_glmnet3),

      replace = TRUE
    )

    pred_matrix_glmnet3[, b] <- (

      pred_glmnet3 +

        sampled_resid3
    )
  }

  #===========================================
  # PI
  #===========================================

  lower_glmnet3 <- apply(

    pred_matrix_glmnet3,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.025
  )

  upper_glmnet3 <- apply(

    pred_matrix_glmnet3,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.975
  )

  #===========================================
  # NONNEGATIVE COUNTS
  #===========================================

  lower_glmnet3 <- pmax(

    0,

    round(lower_glmnet3)
  )

  upper_glmnet3 <- round(

    upper_glmnet3
  )

  #===========================================
  # COVERAGE
  #===========================================

  coverage_glmnet3 <- mean(

    actual3 >= lower_glmnet3 &

    actual3 <= upper_glmnet3
  )

  #===========================================
  # WIDTH
  #===========================================

  width_glmnet3 <- mean(

    upper_glmnet3 -

      lower_glmnet3
  )

  #====================================
  # STORE RESULTS
  #====================================

  results_glmnet3 <- rbind(

    results_glmnet3,

    data.frame(

      Split = i,

      ME = acc_glmnet3[1,"ME"],

      RMSE = acc_glmnet3[1,"RMSE"],

      MAE = acc_glmnet3[1,"MAE"],

      MPE = acc_glmnet3[1,"MPE"],

      MAPE = acc_glmnet3[1,"MAPE"],

      Coverage = coverage_glmnet3,

      Width = width_glmnet3
    )
  )
}
```


```{r}
#====================================
# SUMMARY TABLE
#====================================

summary_glmnet3 <- data.frame(

  Model = "GLMNET3",

  Split = "90%",

  Mean_ME = mean(results_glmnet3$ME,na.rm = TRUE),

  Mean_RMSE = mean(results_glmnet3$RMSE,na.rm = TRUE),

  Mean_MAE = mean(results_glmnet3$MAE,na.rm = TRUE),

  Mean_MPE = mean(results_glmnet3$MPE,na.rm = TRUE),

  Mean_MAPE = mean(results_glmnet3$MAPE,na.rm = TRUE),

  Mean_Coverage = mean(results_glmnet3$Coverage,na.rm = TRUE),

  Mean_Width = mean(results_glmnet3$Width,na.rm = TRUE)
)

summary_glmnet3
```


## neural network
# 70 split
 
```{r}
library(nnet)
library(forecast)

#========================================
# NN1 : 70% SPLITS
# NEURAL NETWORK
# RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP PI
#========================================

results_nn1 <- data.frame()

#========================================
# LOOP
#========================================

for(i in 1:100){

  cat("\n====================================\n")
  cat("NN1 : SPLIT", i, "\n")
  cat("====================================\n")

  #====================================
  # TRAIN AND TEST
  #====================================

  train_1 <- split_70[[i]]$train

  test_1 <- split_70[[i]]$test

  #====================================
  # FACTOR VARIABLES
  #====================================

  train_1$hmo   <- factor(train_1$hmo)

  train_1$died  <- factor(train_1$died)

  train_1$type  <- factor(train_1$type)

  train_1$white <- factor(train_1$white)

  test_1$hmo <- factor(
    test_1$hmo,
    levels = levels(train_1$hmo)
  )

  test_1$died <- factor(
    test_1$died,
    levels = levels(train_1$died)
  )

  test_1$type <- factor(
    test_1$type,
    levels = levels(train_1$type)
  )

  test_1$white <- factor(
    test_1$white,
    levels = levels(train_1$white)
  )

  #====================================
  # MODEL MATRICES
  #====================================

  X_train1 <- model.matrix(

    los ~ hmo + died + type + white,

    data = train_1

  )[,-1]

  X_test1 <- model.matrix(

    los ~ hmo + died + type + white,

    data = test_1

  )[,-1]

  y_train1 <- train_1$los

  y_test1 <- test_1$los

  #====================================
  # SCALE DATA
  #====================================

  X_train1 <- scale(X_train1)

  X_test1 <- scale(

    X_test1,

    center = attr(X_train1,"scaled:center"),

    scale = attr(X_train1,"scaled:scale")
  )

  #====================================
  # FIT NEURAL NETWORK
  #====================================

  nn_model1 <- nnet(

    x = X_train1,

    y = y_train1,

    size = 15,

    linout = TRUE,

    decay = 0.001,

    maxit = 1000,

    trace = FALSE
  )

  #====================================
  # TEST PREDICTIONS
  #====================================

  pred_nn1 <- predict(

    nn_model1,

    X_test1
  )

  pred_nn1 <- as.numeric(
    pred_nn1
  )

  actual1 <- y_test1

  #====================================
  # REMOVE INVALID VALUES
  #====================================

  keep <- complete.cases(

    actual1,

    pred_nn1
  )

  actual1 <- actual1[keep]

  pred_nn1 <- pred_nn1[keep]

  if(length(actual1) < 2) next

  #====================================
  # FORECAST ACCURACY
  #====================================

  acc_nn1 <- forecast::accuracy(

    pred_nn1,

    actual1
  )

  #====================================
  # TRAIN PREDICTIONS
  #====================================

  train_pred_nn1 <- predict(

    nn_model1,

    X_train1
  )

  train_pred_nn1 <- as.numeric(
    train_pred_nn1
  )

  #====================================
  # RESIDUALS
  #====================================

  resid_nn1 <- (

    y_train1 -

      train_pred_nn1
  )

  #====================================
  # NUMBER OF BOOTSTRAPS
  #====================================

  B <- 1000

  pred_matrix_nn1 <- matrix(

    NA,

    nrow = length(pred_nn1),

    ncol = B
  )

  #====================================
  # RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP
  #====================================

  for(b in 1:B){

    sampled_resid1 <- sample(

      resid_nn1,

      size = length(pred_nn1),

      replace = TRUE
    )

    pred_matrix_nn1[,b] <- (

      pred_nn1 +

        sampled_resid1
    )
  }

  #====================================
  # PREDICTION INTERVALS
  #====================================

  lower_nn1 <- apply(

    pred_matrix_nn1,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.025
  )

  upper_nn1 <- apply(

    pred_matrix_nn1,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.975
  )

  #====================================
  # NON-NEGATIVE VALUES
  #====================================

  lower_nn1 <- pmax(

    0,

    lower_nn1
  )

  upper_nn1 <- pmax(

    0,

    upper_nn1
  )

  #====================================
  # COVERAGE
  #====================================

  coverage_nn1 <- mean(

    actual1 >= lower_nn1 &

      actual1 <= upper_nn1
  )

  #====================================
  # WIDTH
  #====================================

  width_nn1 <- mean(

    upper_nn1 -

      lower_nn1
  )

  #====================================
  # STORE EACH SPLIT
  #====================================

  split_nn1 <- data.frame(

    Actual = actual1,

    Predicted = pred_nn1,

    Lower_PI = lower_nn1,

    Upper_PI = upper_nn1
  )

  assign(

    paste0(

      "NN1_SPLIT_",

      i
    ),

    split_nn1
  )

  #====================================
  # STORE RESULTS
  #====================================

  results_nn1 <- rbind(

    results_nn1,

    data.frame(

      Split = i,

      ME = acc_nn1[1,"ME"],

      RMSE = acc_nn1[1,"RMSE"],

      MAE = acc_nn1[1,"MAE"],

      MPE = acc_nn1[1,"MPE"],

      MAPE = acc_nn1[1,"MAPE"],

      Coverage = coverage_nn1,

      Width = width_nn1
    )
  )

  #====================================
  # DISPLAY RESULTS
  #====================================

  cat(

    "\nCoverage :",

    coverage_nn1,

    "\n"
  )

  cat(

    "Width :",

    width_nn1,

    "\n"
  )
}

#========================================
# OVERALL RESULTS
#========================================

summary_nn1 <- data.frame(

  Mean_ME = mean(results_nn1$ME),

  Mean_RMSE = mean(results_nn1$RMSE),

  Mean_MAE = mean(results_nn1$MAE),

  Mean_MPE = mean(results_nn1$MPE),

  Mean_MAPE = mean(results_nn1$MAPE),

  Mean_Coverage = mean(results_nn1$Coverage),

  Mean_Width = mean(results_nn1$Width)
)

print(summary_nn1)



```

```{r}
#====================================
# SUMMARY TABLE
#====================================

summary_nn1 <- data.frame(

  Model = "NN1",

  Split = "70%",

  Mean_ME = mean(
    results_nn1$ME,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_RMSE = mean(
    results_nn1$RMSE,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_MAE = mean(
    results_nn1$MAE,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_MPE = mean(
    results_nn1$MPE,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_MAPE = mean(
    results_nn1$MAPE,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_Coverage = mean(
    results_nn1$Coverage,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_Width = mean(
    results_nn1$Width,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
)

summary_nn1
```


## 80 split
```{r}
library(nnet)
library(forecast)

#========================================
# NN2 : 80% SPLITS
# NEURAL NETWORK
# RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP PI
#========================================

results_nn2 <- data.frame()

#========================================
# LOOP
#========================================

for(i in 1:100){

  cat("\n====================================\n")
  cat("NN2 : SPLIT", i, "\n")
  cat("====================================\n")

  #====================================
  # TRAIN AND TEST
  #====================================

  train_1 <- split_80[[i]]$train

  test_1 <- split_80[[i]]$test

  #====================================
  # FACTOR VARIABLES
  #====================================

  train_1$hmo   <- factor(train_1$hmo)

  train_1$died  <- factor(train_1$died)

  train_1$type  <- factor(train_1$type)

  train_1$white <- factor(train_1$white)

  test_1$hmo <- factor(
    test_1$hmo,
    levels = levels(train_1$hmo)
  )

  test_1$died <- factor(
    test_1$died,
    levels = levels(train_1$died)
  )

  test_1$type <- factor(
    test_1$type,
    levels = levels(train_1$type)
  )

  test_1$white <- factor(
    test_1$white,
    levels = levels(train_1$white)
  )

  #====================================
  # MODEL MATRICES
  #====================================

  X_train1 <- model.matrix(

    los ~ hmo + died + type + white,

    data = train_1

  )[,-1]

  X_test1 <- model.matrix(

    los ~ hmo + died + type + white,

    data = test_1

  )[,-1]

  y_train1 <- train_1$los

  y_test1 <- test_1$los

  #====================================
  # SCALE DATA
  #====================================

  X_train1 <- scale(X_train1)

  X_test1 <- scale(

    X_test1,

    center = attr(X_train1,"scaled:center"),

    scale = attr(X_train1,"scaled:scale")
  )

  #====================================
  # FIT NEURAL NETWORK
  #====================================

  nn_model1 <- nnet(

    x = X_train1,

    y = y_train1,

    size = 15,

    linout = TRUE,

    decay = 0.001,

    maxit = 1000,

    trace = FALSE
  )

  #====================================
  # TEST PREDICTIONS
  #====================================

  pred_nn1 <- predict(

    nn_model1,

    X_test1
  )

  pred_nn1 <- as.numeric(
    pred_nn1
  )

  actual1 <- y_test1

  #====================================
  # REMOVE INVALID VALUES
  #====================================

  keep <- complete.cases(
    actual1,
    pred_nn1
  )

  actual1 <- actual1[keep]

  pred_nn1 <- pred_nn1[keep]

  if(length(actual1) < 2) next

  #====================================
  # FORECAST ACCURACY
  #====================================

  acc_nn1 <- forecast::accuracy(

    pred_nn1,

    actual1
  )

  #====================================
  # TRAIN PREDICTIONS
  #====================================

  train_pred_nn1 <- predict(

    nn_model1,

    X_train1
  )

  train_pred_nn1 <- as.numeric(
    train_pred_nn1
  )

  #====================================
  # RESIDUALS
  #====================================

  resid_nn1 <- (

    y_train1 -

      train_pred_nn1
  )

  #====================================
  # BOOTSTRAP SETTINGS
  #====================================

  B <- 1000

  pred_matrix_nn1 <- matrix(

    NA,

    nrow = length(pred_nn1),

    ncol = B
  )

  #====================================
  # RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP
  #====================================

  for(b in 1:B){

    sampled_resid1 <- sample(

      resid_nn1,

      size = length(pred_nn1),

      replace = TRUE
    )

    pred_matrix_nn1[,b] <- (

      pred_nn1 +

        sampled_resid1
    )
  }

  #====================================
  # PREDICTION INTERVALS
  #====================================

  lower_nn1 <- apply(

    pred_matrix_nn1,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.025
  )

  upper_nn1 <- apply(

    pred_matrix_nn1,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.975
  )

  #====================================
  # NON-NEGATIVE PREDICTIONS
  #====================================

  lower_nn1 <- pmax(
    0,
    lower_nn1
  )

  upper_nn1 <- pmax(
    0,
    upper_nn1
  )

  #====================================
  # COVERAGE
  #====================================

  coverage_nn1 <- mean(

    actual1 >= lower_nn1 &

      actual1 <= upper_nn1
  )

  #====================================
  # WIDTH
  #====================================

  width_nn1 <- mean(

    upper_nn1 -

      lower_nn1
  )

  #====================================
  # STORE SPLIT RESULTS
  #====================================

  split_nn1 <- data.frame(

    Actual = actual1,

    Predicted = pred_nn1,

    Lower_PI = lower_nn1,

    Upper_PI = upper_nn1
  )

  assign(

    paste0(
      "NN2_SPLIT_",
      i
    ),

    split_nn1
  )

  #====================================
  # STORE PERFORMANCE
  #====================================

  results_nn2 <- rbind(

    results_nn2,

    data.frame(

      Split = i,

      ME = acc_nn1[1,"ME"],

      RMSE = acc_nn1[1,"RMSE"],

      MAE = acc_nn1[1,"MAE"],

      MPE = acc_nn1[1,"MPE"],

      MAPE = acc_nn1[1,"MAPE"],

      Coverage = coverage_nn1,

      Width = width_nn1
    )
  )

  #====================================
  # DISPLAY RESULTS
  #====================================

  cat(
    "\nCoverage :",
    coverage_nn1,
    "\n"
  )

  cat(
    "Width :",
    width_nn1,
    "\n"
  )
}


```


```{r}
#====================================
# SUMMARY TABLE
#====================================

summary_nn2 <- data.frame(

  Model = "NN2",

  Split = "80%",

  Mean_ME = mean(
    results_nn2$ME,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_RMSE = mean(
    results_nn2$RMSE,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_MAE = mean(
    results_nn2$MAE,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_MPE = mean(
    results_nn2$MPE,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_MAPE = mean(
    results_nn2$MAPE,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_Coverage = mean(
    results_nn2$Coverage,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_Width = mean(
    results_nn2$Width,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
)

summary_nn2
```

## 90 split

```{r}
library(nnet)
library(forecast)

#========================================
# NN3 : 90% SPLITS
# NEURAL NETWORK
# RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP PI
#========================================

results_nn3 <- data.frame()

#========================================
# LOOP
#========================================

for(i in 1:100){

  cat("\n====================================\n")
  cat("NN3 : SPLIT", i, "\n")
  cat("====================================\n")

  #====================================
  # TRAIN AND TEST
  #====================================

  train_1 <- split_90[[i]]$train

  test_1 <- split_90[[i]]$test

  #====================================
  # FACTOR VARIABLES
  #====================================

  train_1$hmo <- factor(train_1$hmo)

  train_1$died <- factor(train_1$died)

  train_1$type <- factor(train_1$type)

  train_1$white <- factor(train_1$white)

  test_1$hmo <- factor(
    test_1$hmo,
    levels = levels(train_1$hmo)
  )

  test_1$died <- factor(
    test_1$died,
    levels = levels(train_1$died)
  )

  test_1$type <- factor(
    test_1$type,
    levels = levels(train_1$type)
  )

  test_1$white <- factor(
    test_1$white,
    levels = levels(train_1$white)
  )

  #====================================
  # MODEL MATRICES
  #====================================

  X_train1 <- model.matrix(

    los ~ hmo + died + type + white,

    data = train_1

  )[,-1]

  X_test1 <- model.matrix(

    los ~ hmo + died + type + white,

    data = test_1

  )[,-1]

  y_train1 <- train_1$los

  y_test1 <- test_1$los

  #====================================
  # SCALE DATA
  #====================================

  X_train1 <- scale(X_train1)

  X_test1 <- scale(

    X_test1,

    center = attr(X_train1,"scaled:center"),

    scale = attr(X_train1,"scaled:scale")
  )

  #====================================
  # FIT NEURAL NETWORK
  #====================================

  nn_model1 <- nnet(

    x = X_train1,

    y = y_train1,

    size = 15,

    linout = TRUE,

    decay = 0.001,

    maxit = 1000,

    trace = FALSE
  )

  #====================================
  # TEST PREDICTIONS
  #====================================

  pred_nn1 <- predict(

    nn_model1,

    X_test1
  )

  pred_nn1 <- as.numeric(
    pred_nn1
  )

  actual1 <- y_test1

  #====================================
  # REMOVE INVALID VALUES
  #====================================

  keep <- complete.cases(
    actual1,
    pred_nn1
  )

  actual1 <- actual1[keep]

  pred_nn1 <- pred_nn1[keep]

  if(length(actual1) < 2) next

  #====================================
  # FORECAST ACCURACY
  #====================================

  acc_nn1 <- forecast::accuracy(

    pred_nn1,

    actual1
  )

  #====================================
  # TRAIN PREDICTIONS
  #====================================

  train_pred_nn1 <- predict(

    nn_model1,

    X_train1
  )

  train_pred_nn1 <- as.numeric(
    train_pred_nn1
  )

  #====================================
  # RESIDUALS
  #====================================

  resid_nn1 <- (

    y_train1 -

      train_pred_nn1
  )

  #====================================
  # BOOTSTRAP SETTINGS
  #====================================

  B <- 1000

  pred_matrix_nn1 <- matrix(

    NA,

    nrow = length(pred_nn1),

    ncol = B
  )

  #====================================
  # RESIDUAL BOOTSTRAP
  #====================================

  for(b in 1:B){

    sampled_resid1 <- sample(

      resid_nn1,

      size = length(pred_nn1),

      replace = TRUE
    )

    pred_matrix_nn1[,b] <- (

      pred_nn1 +

        sampled_resid1
    )
  }

  #====================================
  # PREDICTION INTERVALS
  #====================================

  lower_nn1 <- apply(

    pred_matrix_nn1,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.025
  )

  upper_nn1 <- apply(

    pred_matrix_nn1,

    1,

    quantile,

    probs = 0.975
  )

  #====================================
  # NON-NEGATIVE PREDICTIONS
  #====================================

  lower_nn1 <- pmax(
    0,
    lower_nn1
  )

  upper_nn1 <- pmax(
    0,
    upper_nn1
  )

  #====================================
  # COVERAGE
  #====================================

  coverage_nn1 <- mean(

    actual1 >= lower_nn1 &

      actual1 <= upper_nn1
  )

  #====================================
  # WIDTH
  #====================================

  width_nn1 <- mean(

    upper_nn1 -

      lower_nn1
  )

  #====================================
  # STORE EACH SPLIT
  #====================================

  split_nn1 <- data.frame(

    Actual = actual1,

    Predicted = pred_nn1,

    Lower_PI = lower_nn1,

    Upper_PI = upper_nn1
  )

  assign(

    paste0(
      "NN3_SPLIT_",
      i
    ),

    split_nn1
  )

  #====================================
  # STORE RESULTS
  #====================================

  results_nn3 <- rbind(

    results_nn3,

    data.frame(

      Split = i,

      ME = acc_nn1[1,"ME"],

      RMSE = acc_nn1[1,"RMSE"],

      MAE = acc_nn1[1,"MAE"],

      MPE = acc_nn1[1,"MPE"],

      MAPE = acc_nn1[1,"MAPE"],

      Coverage = coverage_nn1,

      Width = width_nn1
    )
  )

  #====================================
  # DISPLAY RESULTS
  #====================================

  cat(
    "\nCoverage :",
    coverage_nn1,
    "\n"
  )

  cat(
    "Width :",
    width_nn1,
    "\n"
  )
}


```

```{r}
#====================================
# SUMMARY TABLE
#====================================

summary_nn3 <- data.frame(

  Model = "NN3",

  Split = "90%",

  Mean_ME = mean(
    results_nn3$ME,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_RMSE = mean(
    results_nn3$RMSE,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_MAE = mean(
    results_nn3$MAE,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_MPE = mean(
    results_nn3$MPE,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_MAPE = mean(
    results_nn3$MAPE,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_Coverage = mean(
    results_nn3$Coverage,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ),

  Mean_Width = mean(
    results_nn3$Width,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
)

summary_nn3
```

#ML CIMPARISON
```{r}
library(dplyr)

summary_70_ranked <- comparison_70 %>%
  group_by(Model) %>%
  summarise(
    RMSE = mean(RMSE),
    MAE = mean(MAE),
    MAPE = mean(MAPE),
    Coverage = mean(Coverage),
    Width = mean(Width)
  ) %>%
  mutate(

    Rank_RMSE = rank(RMSE, ties.method = "min"),

    Rank_MAE = rank(MAE, ties.method = "min"),

    Rank_MAPE = rank(MAPE, ties.method = "min"),

    Rank_Coverage = rank(-Coverage, ties.method = "min"),

    Rank_Width = rank(Width, ties.method = "min")

  ) %>%
  mutate(

    Overall_Rank_Score =

      Rank_RMSE +
      Rank_MAE +
      Rank_MAPE +
      Rank_Coverage +
      Rank_Width

  ) %>%
  arrange(Overall_Rank_Score)

summary_70_ranked
library(knitr)

compare_70_final <- summary_70_ranked

latex_table70 <- kable(
  compare_70_final,
  format = "latex",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  align = "c",
  caption = "Comparison of Machine Learning Models under the 70:30 Train--Test Split",
  label = "tab:compare70"
)

writeLines(
  latex_table70,
  "compare_70_ml_medpar.tex"
)

cat(
  "\nTable exported successfully as:\n",
  "compare_70_ml_medpar.tex\n",
  "Upload this file to Overleaf and use:\n",
  "\\input{compare_70_ml_medpar.tex}\n"
)

print(compare_70_final)
```

#80
```{r}
library(dplyr)

#========================================
# ADD MODEL NAMES
#========================================

results_rf2$Model     <- "Random Forest"
results_xgb2$Model    <- "XGBoost"
results_lgb2$Model    <- "LightGBM"
results_glmnet2$Model <- "GLMNET"
results_nn2$Model     <- "Neural Network"

#========================================
# COMBINE RESULTS
#========================================

comparison_80 <- rbind(
  results_rf2,
  results_xgb2,
  results_lgb2,
  results_glmnet2,
  results_nn2
)

#========================================
# SUMMARY AND RANKING
#========================================

summary_80_ranked <- comparison_80 %>%
  group_by(Model) %>%
  summarise(
    RMSE = mean(RMSE),
    MAE = mean(MAE),
    MAPE = mean(MAPE),
    Coverage = mean(Coverage),
    Width = mean(Width)
  ) %>%
  mutate(

    Rank_RMSE = rank(RMSE, ties.method = "min"),

    Rank_MAE = rank(MAE, ties.method = "min"),

    Rank_MAPE = rank(MAPE, ties.method = "min"),

    Rank_Coverage = rank(-Coverage, ties.method = "min"),

    Rank_Width = rank(Width, ties.method = "min")

  ) %>%
  mutate(

    Overall_Rank_Score =

      Rank_RMSE +
      Rank_MAE +
      Rank_MAPE +
      Rank_Coverage +
      Rank_Width

  ) %>%
  arrange(Overall_Rank_Score)

print(summary_80_ranked)
library(knitr)

compare_80_final <- summary_80_ranked

latex_table80 <- kable(
  compare_80_final,
  format = "latex",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  align = "c",
  caption = "Comparison of Machine Learning Models under the 80:20 Train--Test Split",
  label = "tab:compare80"
)

writeLines(
  latex_table80,
  "compare_80_ml_medpar.tex"
)

cat(
  "\nTable exported successfully as:\n",
  "compare_80_ml_medpar.tex\n",
  "Upload this file to Overleaf and use:\n",
  "\\input{compare_80_ml_medpar.tex}\n"
)

print(compare_80_final)
```


#90
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)

#========================================
# ADD MODEL NAMES
#========================================

results_rf3$Model <- "Random Forest"

results_xgb3$Model <- "XGBoost"

results_lgb3$Model <- "LightGBM"

results_glmnet3$Model <- "GLMNET"

results_nn3$Model <- "Neural Network"

#========================================
# COMBINE RESULTS
#========================================

comparison_90 <- rbind(

  results_rf3,

  results_xgb3,

  results_lgb3,

  results_glmnet3,

  results_nn3
)

#========================================
# SUMMARY PERFORMANCE
#========================================

summary_90_ranked <- comparison_90 %>%

  group_by(Model) %>%

  summarise(

    RMSE = mean(RMSE),

    MAE = mean(MAE),

    MAPE = mean(MAPE),

    Coverage = mean(Coverage),

    Width = mean(Width)

  ) %>%

  mutate(

    Rank_RMSE = rank(
      RMSE,
      ties.method = "min"
    ),

    Rank_MAE = rank(
      MAE,
      ties.method = "min"
    ),

    Rank_MAPE = rank(
      MAPE,
      ties.method = "min"
    ),

    Rank_Coverage = rank(
      -Coverage,
      ties.method = "min"
    ),

    Rank_Width = rank(
      Width,
      ties.method = "min"
    )

  ) %>%

  mutate(

    Overall_Rank_Score =

      Rank_RMSE +

      Rank_MAE +

      Rank_MAPE +

      Rank_Coverage +

      Rank_Width

  ) %>%

  arrange(
    Overall_Rank_Score
  )

#========================================
# DISPLAY RESULTS
#========================================

print(summary_90_ranked)

#========================================
# SAVE SUMMARY TABLE
#========================================

compare_90_final <- summary_90_ranked

#========================================
# EXPORT LATEX TABLE
#========================================

latex_table90 <- kable(

  compare_90_final,

  format = "latex",

  booktabs = TRUE,

  align = "c",

  digits = 3,

  caption = "Comparison of Machine Learning Models under the 90:10 Train--Test Split",

  label = "tab:compare90"
)

writeLines(

  latex_table90,

  "compare_90_ml_medpar.tex"
)

cat(

  "\nTable exported successfully as:\n",

  "compare_90_ml_medpar.tex\n",

  "Upload this file to Overleaf and use:\n",

  "\\input{compare_90_ml_medpar.tex}\n"
)


#========================================
# VIEW FINAL TABLE
#========================================

compare_90_final
```
